corrected_statement: Terminal-name resolution chooses COMPONENT IDENTITY over HISTORICAL PATH IDENTITY: all historical coupling is attributed to the repository's final naming scheme. The cochange matrix does NOT reflect paths as they existed at commit time. Defensible for P1; recorded as a choice, not a fact.

max_commit_files=20 is PROVISIONAL, not frozen. It excludes bulk imports/mass refactors which may carry the strongest coupling signal in some repositories. Final value must be justified per target repository, documented, and frozen BEFORE that repository's scoring begins. Changing it after scoring is statistically invalid and prohibited.

Positive control revealed a SOFT circularity channel: size-like features (line counts, append accumulation) partially encode change FREQUENCY, because frequent change physically grows files. ast_metrics detected planted coupling weakly (0.268 vs p95 0.239) through this channel despite deliberately uniform syntax. Consequence: in real-repository scoring, detection by families containing size-correlated features is AMBIGUOUS between structural alignment and change-history leakage, and must be reported with this ambiguity declared. The hard circularity rule (no co-change encoders) is necessary but not sufficient.
supersedes: EXP-001 interpretation limits (circularity rule, implicit completeness)

corrected_statement: ACTIVITY BASELINE added as mandatory second comparison, preregistered before real-repository scoring: a partition built from per-file change-frequency + size alone (history-derived, pair-blind). A candidate family is detected ONLY if capture exceeds BOTH the random-null p95 AND the activity-baseline capture. Detection above null but below activity baseline is classified as leakage (E-003 channel), not structure. The activity baseline is forbidden as a candidate family.
supersedes: P1B scoring_metric (random null as sole comparison)

corrected_statement: MULTIPLICITY RULE: with multiple families x multiple repos at p95, false exceedances are expected by chance. Only the preregistered aggregate criterion (family detected on >=60% of declared repos) is interpretable. Per-repo exceedances are intermediate data, never reportable results.

FILTER FORMULA frozen, closing E-002's forking-paths risk: max_commit_files = min(50, ceil(p95 of non-merge commit file-counts for that repository)). Applied mechanically; no discretionary choice remains.

NEAR-TIE RULE: the activity baseline is a deterministic point value with no distribution; exceeding it by less than the null's own spread is uninterpretable. Three-way classification frozen: DETECTED iff capture > null_p95 AND (capture - activity_capture) > (null_p95 - null_median); LEAKAGE iff > null_p95 but <= activity_capture; UNRESOLVED_ACTIVITY_MARGIN otherwise. Unresolved counts as non-detection for the EXP-001 aggregate criterion. Recorded after the rule's author predicted ast_metrics would fail E-004 and it instead passed by 0.021 — the prediction error is the evidence for the rule.
supersedes: E-004 (binary activity-baseline comparison, no noise band)

corrected_statement: AGGREGATE OUTCOME RULE frozen: per family, success_rate = DETECTED repos / declared repos, with Wilson 95% CI. Outcomes: VALIDATED iff point estimate >= 0.60 (the locked condition); REJECTED iff point < 0.60 AND Wilson upper bound < 0.60; INCONCLUSIVE iff point < 0.60 but upper bound >= 0.60 (insufficient n to decide). EXP-001 concludes positive iff any family is VALIDATED. Repository list must be declared and frozen BEFORE the first real run; additions/removals only via errata recorded before that repository is scored.
supersedes: EXP-001 success condition (point-estimate only, no uncertainty rule)

corrected_statement: MISCLASSIFICATION INCIDENT, recorded as protocol evidence: a rehearsal aggregate (synthetic repos, claim_class=campaign_rehearsal_only, exp001_evidence forbidden) was interpreted as a completed negative EXP-001 result, including a constructed falsification narrative, by a reader fully versed in the protocol. Cause analysis: (1) rehearsal outputs are deliberately schema-identical to evidence outputs; (2) the console summary line printed 'EXP-001: NEGATIVE' without claim scoping — the human-readable channel omitted the metadata the JSON carried. Correction: display layer now carries the claim banner; rule generalized — EVERY human-facing rendering of an artifact must surface claim_class, not only the serialization. Prose discipline does not survive contact with plausible narrative; only surfaced metadata does.
supersedes: session-record interpretation of REHEARSAL_CONCLUSION as EXP-001 outcome; aggregate.py console output

corrected_statement: PILOT ON REAL HISTORY exposed a small-n degeneracy: the p95 index int(0.95*(n-1)) floor-rounds, selecting the MINIMUM commit size at n=2 and biasing low for small histories — on the pilot repository it set max_commit_files=1, emptying the ground truth. Fix (pre-campaign, no real-population data touched): nearest-rank percentile counts[min(n-1, ceil(0.95*n)-1)]. Negligible for wave-1 scale (thousands of commits); decisive for short histories. Origin note: this erratum exists because an untested incapacity claim ('no immediate real test is possible') was challenged and falsified — the immediate real test found this bug within one run.

PLATFORM-DEPENDENT TEXT DECODING, exposed by repository 1 (psf/requests) first contact: encoder/scorer file reads used locale-default open() and all git subprocess output used locale-default text=True. On Windows (cp1252) this crashed on tests/test_requests.py byte 0x8f (valid UTF-8); on POSIX (UTF-8) the identical code succeeds -- instrument output was a function of host locale, a cross-platform determinism violation. The rehearsal could not catch it: synthetic repos were pure ASCII (external-validity lesson, recorded). Fix: every repo-content read and git-output decode is now explicit encoding='utf-8', errors='replace'; undecodable or unparseable repo content degrades deterministically (encoders catch SyntaxError/ValueError/OSError and emit their declared zero/skip signature) instead of crashing. Project-internal JSON I/O is outside this defect class (ensure_ascii output is ASCII). COUNTS AS INSTRUMENT ERRATUM 1 OF 3 against the UPDATE_COMMITMENTS section-C budget. Repository 1 emitted no scoring artifact; it rescores from scratch post-fix.

FILTER-ALARM CALIBRATION: the 'filter < 5' suspend trigger fired on repository 1 with E-006 filter=3. Direct verification against the raw distribution (4843 non-merge commits; file-count p50=1, p90=2, p95=3, max=86; 74% of commits touch one file) confirms filter=3 is the CORRECT nearest-rank p95: small-commit repository, formula behaved as designed. The fixed threshold was calibrated on synthetic rehearsal repos and is superseded: a filter alarm now resolves by mandatory direct distribution verification recorded alongside the repo's artifacts; a verified-correct value closes the alarm without suspension. Classified as commitment-rule calibration, NOT instrument defect (no instrument code changed by this erratum); if the operator judges it instrument-class at countersign, the section-C count becomes 2 of 3.
supersedes: UPDATE_COMMITMENTS section A implausible-metric row (fixed 'filter < 5' threshold)

corrected_statement: SLUG CORRECTION for R038: REPO_DECLARATION_exp002.json listed 'psf/cryptography' which does not exist on GitHub (clone returns 404). The intended repository is 'pyca/cryptography' (Python Cryptographic Authority), the canonical Python cryptography library. The PSF org does not host this project. Discovered at R038 clone attempt during batch 3. This is a declaration typo (wrong org prefix), not mechanical unavailability. The repository pyca/cryptography is public, not archived, Python-primary, and satisfies all POPULATION_CRITERIA.md inclusion criteria. REPO_DECLARATION_exp002.json and run_batch_exp002.ps1 corrected to pyca/cryptography. Declaration hash recomputed over corrected slug list. NOT an instrument erratum; section-C erratum budget unchanged (3/3 remaining). NOT a mechanical unavailability event; RQ slots unchanged.

VENDORED-TREE EXCLUSION for pypa/pip: pip's repository contains src/pip/_vendor/, a subtree of vendored copies of external packages (requests, urllib3, certifi, etc.) not authored or maintained by the pip team. Including this subtree would (a) inject co-change pairs driven by upstream-sync commits rather than pip's own coupling structure, (b) attribute file ownership to maintainers of vendored packages rather than pip contributors, and (c) introduce dep_graph edges between vendored packages that are absent in every other study population member. No other wave-1 repository carries a vendored subtree of comparable scale. To preserve population-level comparability -- the ceteris-paribus condition that makes cross-repo aggregation interpretable -- src/pip/_vendor/* is excluded from the tracked-file set for pip's scoring via --exclude-glob 'src/pip/_vendor/*'. This exclusion is declared BEFORE pip is scored; it was not chosen after observing pip's metrics. The --exclude-glob parameter was already present in run_study.py and corpus_builder.py; no instrument code changes. Classified as commitment-rule calibration (population comparability scope decision), NOT an instrument defect; section-C erratum budget remains at 1 of 3.
supersedes: REPO_DECLARATION implicit scope assumption (tracked files = all files in repo)

corrected_statement: DISPLAY-ONLY SYNTAX FIX in compute_moderators_exp005.py: f-string at line 149 used conditional format specifier syntax ':.4f if m1 else N/A!r' which is invalid in Python 3.12 and raises ValueError at runtime. Fix: pre-compute display strings m1_s and m2_s outside the f-string before the print call. The fix affects only console output; no computation is changed (Gini formula, M2 formula, ground_truth parsing, output JSON structure all identical). Discovered at first PowerShell execution attempt, before any moderator values were produced. Filed pre-execution per protocol. NOT an instrument erratum; section-C erratum budget unchanged (3/3 remaining). HYPOTHESIS_exp005.md updated with new script hash.
supersedes: compute_moderators_exp005.py line 149 f-string format specifier (display only)

corrected_statement: ENCODING FIX in compute_moderators_exp005.py git_author_commit_counts(): subprocess.run() used text=True without explicit encoding, defaulting to cp1252 on Windows. Non-ASCII bytes in author email data (byte 0x9d in matplotlib clone git log output) caused UnicodeDecodeError in the subprocess reader thread, leaving result.stdout as None and crashing at result.stdout.splitlines(). Fix: add encoding='utf-8', errors='replace' to subprocess.run() call, and guard result.stdout is None. Identical defect class to E-011 (instrument locale-default decoding), but E-011 covered only the instrument's encoders and corpus_builder; compute_moderators scripts were not in E-011 scope. Discovered at first full execution attempt after E-015 fix, before moderators_exp005.json was written (output JSON not produced by the crashing run). Filed pre-result per protocol. NOT an instrument erratum; section-C erratum budget unchanged (3/3 remaining). HYPOTHESIS_exp005.md updated with new script hash.

SLUG CORRECTION for C007: REPO_DECLARATION_exp101.json listed invoke/invoke which does not exist on GitHub (clone returns 404). The correct org prefix is pyinvoke (not invoke). The intended repository is pyinvoke/invoke (Jeff Forcier task execution library at github.com/pyinvoke/invoke). Discovered at C007 clone attempt during batch run. Same defect class as E-014. NOT an instrument erratum; section-C erratum budget unchanged (3/3 remaining). REPO_DECLARATION_exp101.json and run_batch_exp101.ps1 corrected to pyinvoke/invoke. Declaration hash recomputed. NOT a mechanical unavailability event; replacement queue slots unchanged.

M3 (Galois closure diameter) added as OBSERVATIONAL METADATA ONLY to compute_moderators_exp101.py and moderators_exp101.json. M3 = mean_a(|gamma(alpha_maj({a}))|)/|Authors| where alpha_maj selects files where author a holds strictly more than 50% of commits; gamma(T) = all authors touching any file in T. The 0.4 pseudo-T threshold is informational only and carries no classification weight in EXP-101. T-cell definition for EXP-101 remains frozen: M1 > 0.858675 AND M2 < 7.702891 per HYPOTHESIS_exp101.md commit 5864fb2. M3 is computed using the same observation window (after_ts, before_ts) as M1/M2 via a single additional git log --name-only pass. Stored as 'm3_galois' and 'pseudo_tcell_m3' fields in each moderators record. The EXP-101 primary E-008 test (Wilson CI on M1^M2 T-cell count) is unaffected. M3 threshold will be preregistered for EXP-102 based on EXP-101 observed distribution. Filed pre-execution: compute_moderators_exp101.py has not been run yet. NOT an instrument erratum (scorer.py unchanged); section-C erratum budget unchanged (3/3 remaining).

SLUG CORRECTION for C012: REPO_DECLARATION_exp101.json listed pyzmq/pyzmq which does not exist on GitHub (clone returns 404 'Repository not found'). The correct org prefix is zeromq (not pyzmq). The intended repository is zeromq/pyzmq (Python bindings for ZeroMQ, Min Ragan-Kelley primary author, github.com/zeromq/pyzmq). Discovered at C012 clone attempt during batch run. Same defect class as E-014 and E-017. NOT an instrument erratum; section-C erratum budget unchanged (3/3 remaining). REPO_DECLARATION_exp101.json and run_batch_exp101.ps1 corrected to zeromq/pyzmq. Declaration hash recomputed. NOT a mechanical unavailability event; replacement queue slots unchanged.

SLUG CORRECTION for C015: REPO_DECLARATION_exp101.json listed gitpython/gitpython which does not exist on GitHub (clone returns 404 'Repository not found'). The correct org is gitpython-developers (not gitpython) and the repo name is capitalized GitPython. The intended repository is gitpython-developers/GitPython (Sebastian Thiel primary author, github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython). Discovered at C015 clone attempt during batch run. Same defect class as E-014, E-017, E-019. NOT an instrument erratum; section-C erratum budget unchanged (3/3 remaining). REPO_DECLARATION_exp101.json and run_batch_exp101.ps1 corrected to gitpython-developers/GitPython. Declaration hash recomputed. NOT a mechanical unavailability event; replacement queue slots unchanged.
